FR-020.title: Readable header content uses body/primary tokens; muted only for metadata

context: area: src/features/specs/livingSpecsModel.ts (existing coverage + drift computation to reuse)
constraint: never interpolate user data into HTML attributes via innerHTML
constraint: feature-spec header behavior must not change
intent: Make the living-spec viewer header show the author's real title, say DRAFT once, and surface the capability facts (size, coverage, drift, claimed files, location) that already exist elsewhere.
expectations: No change to the feature-spec header (branch, created date, phases, task progress)
The in-body [DRAFT] banner is not removed
No new computation of coverage or drift — reuse the sidebar's
The header stays read-only; it does not edit capabilities or config
approach: Derive the living-spec title from the document's own first heading (stripping a trailing Living Spec suffix) and turn off the presentational capitalization for it. Drop the badge tooltip when it would only repeat the badge. Add a facts row for living specs — requirement and scenario counts, coverage, drift, claimed file patterns, spec location — sourcing coverage and drift from the sidebar's own livingSpecsModel functions rather than a second derivation, and pushing the git-dependent fields in after first paint so the panel never waits.

decisions.decision: Read the title from the document H1 rather than the folder slug
decisions.why: The heading is the only place the real name exists; a slug cannot round-trip product casing like SpecKit
decisions.rejected: Storing a display name in companion.yml — duplicates the name and drifts

decisions.why: Makes sidebar and viewer disagreement structurally impossible; the functions are already absent-on-failure
decisions.rejected: A new shared service layer — indirection with no new behavior

decisions.decision: Render the header before the drift check resolves, then push coverage and drift as a nav-state update
decisions.why: Drift runs two git commands; a blank panel on every open is a worse regression than a late marker
decisions.rejected: Blocking first paint on the health call

decisions.decision: Turn off text-transform: capitalize for heading-derived titles
decisions.why: The stylesheet would otherwise re-mangle SpecKit back to Speckit and the fix would look broken
decisions.rejected: Removing the capitalization entirely — regresses feature specs, which really are slugs

decisions.decision: Show three claimed patterns inline plus a +N more overflow
decisions.why: Answers which files without letting a many-pattern capability push the document below the fold
decisions.rejected: Showing a bare count — fails the ticket question, which is which files not how many

decisions.decision: Split livingSpecHeading (returns null when there is none) out from livingSpecTitle
decisions.why: The title and the skip-capitalization flag are then one derivation read twice, instead of the flag guessing from title !== fallback
decisions.rejected: Comparing the derived title against the fallback — wrong whenever a heading happens to equal the slug

decisions.decision: Read the title from the spec tier, not the tier currently on screen
decisions.why: The title names the capability; switching to the Architecture tab should not change it
decisions.rejected: Using whichever tier is rendering — the title would flicker between tabs

decisions.decision: Give the spec path its own row rather than appending it to the Covers row
decisions.why: A capability that claims nothing still has a location; nesting it inside the Covers row would hide it
decisions.rejected: One combined row with conditional placement

plan.summary: Header title from the document heading, tooltip dropped when redundant, and a living-spec facts row reusing the sidebar coverage/drift computation
plan.key_finding: The header title carries text-transform: capitalize — reading the H1 without also disabling that would have silently re-mangled the name

tasks.summary: 26 tasks across 7 phases — shared types, then one phase per user story, then docs and stories in a fully parallel polish wave

implement.summary: Living-spec header now shows the authored title, one DRAFT, and the capability facts — counts, coverage, drift, claimed patterns and location — reusing the sidebar computation

T001.did: Added the LivingHeaderMeta fact bundle plus livingMeta/titleFromHeading on the extension-side navigation state

T002.did: Mirrored the fact bundle and the two new navigation-state fields on the webview side

T003.did: Threaded the fact bundle and the heading-title flag through the HTML generator into the initial navigation state

T004.did: Added 11 title-derivation cases: suffix variants, casing, frontmatter, fenced blocks, emphasis, and every fallback path

T005.did: Added livingSpecTitle — first H1 outside fences and frontmatter, Living Spec suffix stripped, fallback when unusable

T006.did: Living panel title, tab label and header title now come from the spec tier's heading whichever tier is on screen

T007.did: Added the authored-title modifier that turns off slug capitalization, plus the truncation trio on the title

T008.did: Header applies the authored-title class when the title came from a document heading

T009.did: Added the SpecHeader test suite: tooltip omission, authored title, facts row, claimed files and location

T010.did: Badge sets hover text only when a created date adds something; otherwise the attribute is omitted

T011.did: Added 13 fact-derivation cases: counts, fence handling, absence-not-zero, capability resolution, both storage layouts, and the shared-health reuse

T012.did: Added countLivingFacts — distinct requirement ids and numbered scenarios, both absent when none are found

T013.did: Added buildLivingHeaderMeta — resolves the owning capability from the project config and folds in the counts, synchronously

T014.did: Provider builds the fact bundle when a living spec renders and passes it into the initial navigation state

T015.did: Header renders the facts row — counts, coverage, drift with its accessible label — using the pinned wording

T016.did: Styled the facts row, pattern chips and location chip; body tokens for values, muted only for the path, every chip truncates

T017.did: Header lists up to three claimed patterns plus an overflow chip carrying the rest

T018.did: Header shows the spec path with the sidebar's own central-versus-colocated explanation

T019.did: Coverage and drift resolve through the sidebar's readCapabilityHealth after first paint and arrive as a navigation-state update

T020.did: Pinned the reuse: health comes from the shared call, stays absent when it yields nothing, and never rejects

T021.did: Added seven living-spec stories: everything known, draft, no coverage tier, drifted, many patterns, long colocated title, and nothing determinable

T022.did: Reading Specs now describes the living-spec header and the badge hover rule

T023.did: Documented the badge hover rule and the full living-spec header, including why the authored title must not be re-cased

T024.did: Noted that the viewer shows the sidebar's own coverage and drift, from one computation

T025.did: Added the Unreleased entry: useful living-spec header, real title, one DRAFT

T026.did: Compile, 1637 tests across 120 suites, and a package build all green; every new test drift-proofed against a reverted behavior

verified.what: Drift-proofing — every new test reverted against old behavior
